items.content_statement: understand and use place value for decimals, measures and integers of any size
items.classification_rationale: hard prerequisite sequence - place value understanding must precede advanced number operations

items.content_statement: order positive and negative integers, decimals and fractions; use the number line as a model for ordering of the real numbers; use the symbols =, ≠, <, >, ≤, ≥
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with prerequisite sequence - ordering requires place value understanding
prerequisite_lts: understand and use place value for decimals, measures and integers of any size

items.content_statement: use the concepts and vocabulary of prime numbers, factors (or divisors), multiples, common factors, common multiples, highest common factor, lowest common multiple, prime factorisation, including using product notation and the unique factorisation property
items.classification_rationale: conceptual knowledge with hierarchical structure - prime factorisation builds on factor understanding

items.content_statement: use the four operations, including formal written methods, applied to integers, decimals, proper and improper fractions, and mixed numbers, all both positive and negative
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with prerequisite sequence - operations with complex numbers require place value understanding
prerequisite_lts: understand and use place value for decimals, measures and integers of any size

items.content_statement: use conventional notation for the priority of operations, including brackets, powers, roots and reciprocals
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with fixed sequence - order of operations must be learned before complex calculations

items.content_statement: recognise and use relationships between operations including inverse operations
items.classification_rationale: conceptual understanding with prerequisite sequence - inverse relationships build on operation mastery
prerequisite_lts: use the four operations, including formal written methods, applied to integers, decimals, proper and improper fractions, and mixed numbers, all both positive and negative

items.content_statement: use integer powers and associated real roots (square, cube and higher), recognise powers of 2, 3, 4, 5 and distinguish between exact representations of roots and their decimal approximations
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with prerequisite sequence - powers and roots require operation understanding
prerequisite_lts: use the four operations, including formal written methods, applied to integers, decimals, proper and improper fractions, and mixed numbers, all both positive and negative

items.content_statement: interpret and compare numbers in standard form A x 10n 1≤A<10, where n is a positive or negative integer or zero
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with prerequisite sequence - standard form requires place value and power understanding
prerequisite_lts: understand and use place value for decimals, measures and integers of any size
use integer powers and associated real roots (square, cube and higher), recognise powers of 2, 3, 4, 5 and distinguish between exact representations of roots and their decimal approximations

items.content_statement: work interchangeably with terminating decimals and their corresponding fractions
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with prerequisite sequence - decimal-fraction conversion requires place value understanding
prerequisite_lts: understand and use place value for decimals, measures and integers of any size

items.content_statement: define percentage as 'number of parts per hundred', interpret percentages and percentage changes as a fraction or a decimal, interpret these multiplicatively, express one quantity as a percentage of another, compare two quantities using percentages, and work with percentages greater than 100%
items.classification_rationale: conceptual understanding with prerequisite sequence - percentage understanding builds on fraction and decimal knowledge
prerequisite_lts: work interchangeably with terminating decimals and their corresponding fractions

items.content_statement: interpret fractions and percentages as operators
items.classification_rationale: conceptual understanding with prerequisite sequence - operator interpretation requires percentage understanding
prerequisite_lts: define percentage as 'number of parts per hundred', interpret percentages and percentage changes as a fraction or a decimal, interpret these multiplicatively, express one quantity as a percentage of another, compare two quantities using percentages, and work with percentages greater than 100%

items.content_statement: use standard units of mass, length, time, money and other measures, including with decimal quantities
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with clear steps and criteria for correct application

items.content_statement: round numbers and measures to an appropriate degree of accuracy [for example, to a number of decimal places or significant figures]
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with clear steps and criteria for correct application

items.content_statement: use approximation through rounding to estimate answers and calculate possible resulting errors expressed using inequality notation a<x≤b
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with clear steps and criteria for correct application

items.content_statement: use a calculator and other technologies to calculate results accurately and then interpret them appropriately
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with clear steps and criteria for correct application

items.content_statement: appreciate the infinite nature of the sets of integers, real and rational numbers
items.classification_rationale: conceptual understanding of mathematical properties that can be assessed through explanation or demonstration

items.content_statement: use and interpret algebraic notation, including: ab in place of a × b, 3y in place of y + y + y and 3 × y, a2 in place of a × a, a3 in place of a × a × a; a2b in place of a × a × b, a/b in place of a ÷ b, coefficients written as fractions rather than as decimals, brackets
items.classification_rationale: hard prerequisite sequence - algebraic notation must be understood before manipulation

items.content_statement: substitute numerical values into formulae and expressions, including scientific formulae
items.classification_rationale: hard prerequisite sequence - procedural skill requiring prior notation knowledge

items.content_statement: understand and use the concepts and vocabulary of expressions, equations, inequalities, terms and factors
items.classification_rationale: hard prerequisite sequence - conceptual understanding builds on notation

items.content_statement: simplify and manipulate algebraic expressions to maintain equivalence by: collecting like terms, multiplying a single term over a bracket, taking out common factors, expanding products of two or more binomials
items.classification_rationale: hard prerequisite sequence - manipulation requires prior understanding of notation and concepts
prerequisite_lts: understand and use the concepts and vocabulary of expressions, equations, inequalities, terms and factors

items.content_statement: understand and use standard mathematical formulae; rearrange formulae to change the subject
items.classification_rationale: hard prerequisite sequence - formula manipulation requires prior algebraic manipulation skills
prerequisite_lts: simplify and manipulate algebraic expressions to maintain equivalence

items.content_statement: model situations or procedures by translating them into algebraic expressions or formulae and by using graphs
items.classification_rationale: hard prerequisite sequence - modeling requires prior understanding of expressions and formulae
prerequisite_lts: understand and use the concepts and vocabulary of expressions, equations, inequalities, terms and factors

items.content_statement: use algebraic methods to solve linear equations in one variable (including all forms that require rearrangement)
items.classification_rationale: hard prerequisite sequence - equation solving requires prior manipulation skills
prerequisite_lts: simplify and manipulate algebraic expressions to maintain equivalence

items.content_statement: work with coordinates in all four quadrants
items.classification_rationale: hard prerequisite sequence - coordinate work is foundational for graphing

items.content_statement: recognise, sketch and produce graphs of linear and quadratic functions of one variable with appropriate scaling, using equations in x and y and the Cartesian plane
items.classification_rationale: hard prerequisite sequence - graphing functions requires coordinate understanding and algebraic concepts
prerequisite_lts: work with coordinates in all four quadrants
understand and use the concepts and vocabulary of expressions, equations, inequalities, terms and factors

items.content_statement: interpret mathematical relationships both algebraically and graphically
items.classification_rationale: hard prerequisite sequence - interpretation requires prior graphing and algebraic skills
prerequisite_lts: recognise, sketch and produce graphs of linear and quadratic functions
simplify and manipulate algebraic expressions to maintain equivalence

items.content_statement: reduce a given linear equation in two variables to the standard form y = mx + c
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with fixed steps - Type 1

items.content_statement: calculate and interpret gradients and intercepts of graphs of such linear equations numerically, graphically and algebraically
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with fixed steps - Type 1

items.content_statement: use linear and quadratic graphs to estimate values of y for given values of x and vice versa and to find approximate solutions of simultaneous linear equations
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with fixed steps - Type 1

items.content_statement: find approximate solutions to contextual problems from given graphs of a variety of functions, including piece-wise linear, exponential and reciprocal graphs
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with fixed steps - Type 1

items.content_statement: generate terms of a sequence from either a term-to-term or a position-to-term rule
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with fixed steps - Type 1

items.content_statement: recognise arithmetic sequences and find the nth term
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with fixed steps - Type 1

items.content_statement: recognise geometric sequences and appreciate other sequences that arise
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with fixed steps - Type 1

items.content_statement: change freely between related standard units [for example time, length, area, volume/capacity, mass]
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with clear steps that can be demonstrated and assessed

strand.strand_name: Ratio, proportion and rates of change

items.content_statement: use scale factors, scale diagrams and maps
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with clear steps that can be demonstrated and assessed

strand.strand_name: Ratio, proportion and rates of change

items.content_statement: express one quantity as a fraction of another, where the fraction is less than 1 and greater than 1
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with clear steps that can be demonstrated and assessed

strand.strand_name: Ratio, proportion and rates of change

items.content_statement: use ratio notation, including reduction to simplest form
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with clear steps that can be demonstrated and assessed

strand.strand_name: Ratio, proportion and rates of change

items.content_statement: divide a given quantity into two parts in a given part:part or part:whole ratio; express the division of a quantity into two parts as a ratio
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with clear steps that can be demonstrated and assessed

strand.strand_name: Ratio, proportion and rates of change

items.content_statement: understand that a multiplicative relationship between two quantities can be expressed as a ratio or a fraction
items.classification_rationale: conceptual understanding that builds on prerequisite knowledge of ratios and fractions

strand.strand_name: Ratio, proportion and rates of change

items.content_statement: relate the language of ratios and the associated calculations to the arithmetic of fractions and to linear functions
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill connecting mathematical concepts with clear steps

strand.strand_name: Ratio, proportion and rates of change

items.content_statement: solve problems involving percentage change, including: percentage increase, decrease and original value problems and simple interest in financial mathematics
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with clear steps that can be demonstrated and assessed

strand.strand_name: Ratio, proportion and rates of change

items.content_statement: solve problems involving direct and inverse proportion, including graphical and algebraic representations
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with clear steps that can be demonstrated and assessed

strand.strand_name: Ratio, proportion and rates of change

items.content_statement: use compound units such as speed, unit pricing and density to solve problems
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with clear steps that can be demonstrated and assessed

strand.strand_name: Ratio, proportion and rates of change

items.content_statement: derive and apply formulae to calculate and solve problems involving: perimeter and area of triangles, parallelograms, trapezia, volume of cuboids (including cubes) and other prisms (including cylinders)
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with fixed sequence - formula derivation and application follows prerequisite mathematical knowledge

items.content_statement: calculate and solve problems involving: perimeters of 2-D shapes (including circles), areas of circles and composite shapes
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with fixed sequence - calculation procedures follow prerequisite mathematical knowledge

items.content_statement: draw and measure line segments and angles in geometric figures, including interpreting scale drawings
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with fixed sequence - geometric construction follows prerequisite knowledge of geometric principles

items.content_statement: derive and use the standard ruler and compass constructions (perpendicular bisector of a line segment, constructing a perpendicular to a given line from/at a given point, bisecting a given angle); recognise and use the perpendicular distance from a point to a line as the shortest distance to the line
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with fixed sequence - geometric constructions follow prerequisite knowledge of geometric principles

items.content_statement: describe, sketch and draw using conventional terms and notations: points, lines, parallel lines, perpendicular lines, right angles, regular polygons, and other polygons that are reflectively and rotationally symmetric
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with fixed sequence - geometric representation follows prerequisite knowledge of geometric vocabulary and conventions

items.content_statement: use the standard conventions for labelling the sides and angles of triangle ABC, and know and use the criteria for congruence of triangles
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge - mathematical conventions and criteria that must be understood before application

items.content_statement: derive and illustrate properties of triangles, quadrilaterals, circles, and other plane figures using appropriate language and technologies
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with fixed sequence - deriving properties follows prerequisite knowledge of geometric principles

items.content_statement: identify properties of, and describe the results of, translations, rotations and reflections applied to given figures
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with fixed sequence - identifying transformation properties follows prerequisite knowledge of transformations

items.content_statement: identify and construct congruent triangles, and construct similar shapes by enlargement, with and without coordinate grids
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with fixed sequence - construction procedures follow prerequisite knowledge of congruence and similarity

items.content_statement: apply the properties of angles at a point, angles at a point on a straight line, vertically opposite angles
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with fixed sequence - applying angle properties follows prerequisite knowledge of angle relationships

items.content_statement: understand and use the relationship between parallel lines and alternate and corresponding angles
items.classification_rationale: conceptual understanding with fixed sequence - understanding angle relationships is prerequisite to application

items.content_statement: derive and use the sum of angles in a triangle and use it to deduce the angle sum in any polygon, and to derive properties of regular polygons
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with fixed sequence - deriving angle sums follows prerequisite knowledge of geometric principles

items.content_statement: apply angle facts, triangle congruence, similarity and properties of quadrilaterals to derive results about angles and sides, including Pythagoras' Theorem, and use known results to obtain simple proofs
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with fixed sequence - applying geometric facts to derive results follows prerequisite knowledge of those facts

items.content_statement: use Pythagoras' Theorem and trigonometric ratios in similar triangles to solve problems involving right-angled triangles
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with fixed sequence - using theorems and ratios follows prerequisite knowledge of those mathematical concepts

items.content_statement: use the properties of faces, surfaces, edges and vertices of cubes, cuboids, prisms, cylinders, pyramids, cones and spheres to solve problems in 3-D
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with fixed sequence - using 3D properties follows prerequisite knowledge of 3D geometric concepts

items.content_statement: interpret mathematical relationships both algebraically and geometrically
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with fixed sequence - interpreting relationships follows prerequisite knowledge of algebraic and geometric concepts

items.content_statement: record, describe and analyse the frequency of outcomes of simple probability experiments involving randomness, fairness, equally and unequally likely outcomes, using appropriate language and the 0-1 probability scale
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with clear steps and criteria - Type 1

items.content_statement: the probabilities of all possible outcomes sum to 1
items.classification_rationale: mathematical concept/principle to be understood - Type 1

items.content_statement: enumerate sets and unions/intersections of sets systematically, using tables, grids and
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with systematic method - Type 1

items.content_statement: describe, interpret and compare observed distributions of a single variable through appropriate graphical representation involving discrete, continuous and grouped data; and appropriate measures of central tendency (mean, mode, median) and spread (range, consideration of outliers)
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with prerequisite sequence - must understand data types and statistical measures before applying them

items.content_statement: construct and interpret appropriate tables, charts, and diagrams, including frequency tables, bar charts, pie charts, and pictograms for categorical data, and vertical line (or bar) charts for ungrouped and grouped numerical data
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with prerequisite sequence - must understand data types and chart construction principles before applying them

items.content_statement: describe simple mathematical relationships between two variables (bivariate data) in observational and experimental contexts and illustrate using scatter graphs
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with prerequisite sequence - must understand variable relationships and scatter plot construction before applying them
